name: Linear Scan
summary: Walk every element and keep the smallest seen — correct but O(n), ignoring the sorted-rotated structure.
visualizationIntent: init: show the whole array; i starts at index 0 and ans holds the first element as the running minimum. loop: i walks left to right, one cell at a time (the current cell). check: compare nums[i] against the running minimum ans. update: when nums[i] is smaller, ans drops to it and that cell becomes the new highlighted minimum. return: the cell holding ans is the answer; every cell has been visited.

lineExplanations.3: Count the elements in the array.
lineExplanations.4: Start the running minimum as the first element.
lineExplanations.6: Keep scanning while there are elements left.
lineExplanations.7: Is the current element smaller than the best seen so far?
lineExplanations.8: If so, update the running minimum to this element.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def findMin(self, nums): nums is the rotated sorted array.
syntaxExplanations.4: ans = nums[0] seeds the running minimum with the first element.
syntaxExplanations.5: i = 0 starts the scan at the first index.
syntaxExplanations.6: while i < n: loops until every element is checked.
syntaxExplanations.7: if nums[i] < ans: compares the current element to the best so far.
syntaxExplanations.8: ans = nums[i] lowers the running minimum.
syntaxExplanations.9: i = i + 1 moves to the next element.
syntaxExplanations.10: return ans hands back the minimum.
